content.thinking:
Let me analyze the residual finding and evaluate its user impact.

The finding is about an omitted image reference (`send_sync.png`) in the knowledge file. The source RST explicitly shows this image with a caption explaining that it illustrates how group IDs link test case rows to message data tables.

Let me assess the impact:

1. The knowledge file already has extensive textual description of the group ID concept - it explains:
   - How `expectedMessage`/`responseMessage` fields contain group IDs
   - How these group IDs correspond to identifiers in the data tables
   - The identifier format (EXPECTED_REQUEST_HEADER_MESSAGES[グループID]=リクエストID etc.)
   - The table structure with all columns described

2. The image `send_sync.png` would provide a visual representation of how group IDs connect test cases to message tables in Excel format, while the knowledge file already includes one image reference (`send_sync_example.png`) for the request body example.

3. Since the textual content in s2 covers the group ID relationship concept fairly thoroughly, the missing `send_sync.png` represents a visual aid that's not critical for understanding the concept, though it would have reinforced the linking mechanism visually.

Looking at the Phase E logs, the knowledge file content remains unchanged from before—the fix wasn't actually applied despite being identified. For user impact, the group ID mechanism is already well-explained through text, so AI agents can understand it without the image. I'd rate this as low impact since the textual description is comprehensive enough, even though the visual confirmation would have been helpful.

input.reason: The single residual finding concerns a missing image reference (send_sync.png) that illustrates the group ID relationship in Excel test data. The knowledge file's s2 section already provides a comprehensive textual description of the group ID mechanism: identifier formats (EXPECTED_REQUEST_HEADER_MESSAGES[グループID]=リクエストID etc.), the correspondence between test case fields and table identifiers, and all table columns. An AI agent reading this file can fully explain and guide users on the group ID concept without the image. The omission is a supplementary visual aid, not load-bearing information. User impact is low.

findings_assessment.location: s2 — テストデータの書き方
findings_assessment.justification: The missing send_sync.png reference is a visual aid illustrating how group IDs link test case rows to message data tables. However, the s2 section already contains a thorough textual description of this relationship: it explains that expectedMessage/responseMessage fields contain group IDs that correspond to identifiers in the data tables, lists all four identifier formats (EXPECTED_REQUEST_HEADER_MESSAGES, EXPECTED_REQUEST_BODY_MESSAGES, RESPONSE_HEADER_MESSAGES, RESPONSE_BODY_MESSAGES), and describes the full table structure. The image adds no information not already present in text. The Phase E agent left the knowledge file unchanged from its input (s2 content is identical in both D and E logs), indicating the fix was simply not applied — likely because the agent determined the textual coverage was sufficient for the minor severity finding.
